steps.title: Recall the standard form of a circle
steps.content: A circle with center $(h,k)$ and radius $r$ has equation $$(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$$ With center $(-5,1)$: $h=-5$, $k=1$, so the equation starts as $$(x+5)^2+(y-1)^2=r^2$$

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$(x-5)^2+(y+1)^2=5$': Center would be $(5,-1)$, not $(-5,1)$, and $r^2=5\neq 25$. ✗
  • Choice B — '$(x+5)^2+(y-1)^2=5$': Center $(-5,1)$ is correct, but $r^2=5\neq 25$. ✗
  • Choice C — '$(x-5)^2+(y+1)^2=25$': $r^2=25$ is correct, but center would be $(5,-1)$, not $(-5,1)$. ✗
  • Choice D — '$(x+5)^2+(y-1)^2=25$': Center $(-5,1)$ and $r^2=25$ both correct. That matches choice D — D is correct. ✓
